title: Frame length field allows 0-byte body which violates minimum
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/frame.py:156-166

The from_bytes parsing checks:

    if len(data) < 1:
        raise FrameError('frame is empty')
    length = data[0]
    body = data[1:]
    if len(body) != length:
        raise FrameError(...)
    if length < 4:
        raise FrameError(f'frame body too short: {length} bytes')

A frame with data = b'\x00' would:
1. Pass 'len(data) < 1' check (length is 1)
2. Set length = 0
3. Set body = b'' (empty)
4. Pass 'len(body) != length' check (0 == 0)
5. Fail 'length < 4' check

So this is correctly handled. But what about length = 1, 2, or 3?

data = b'\x01\x00': length=1, body=b'\x00', len(body)==length, then fails length<4.

This is correct. No bug here, just verifying the edge cases are handled.

Actually, this is NOT a bug. Closing/withdrawing this finding.
close_reason: False positive - after analysis, the edge cases are correctly handled by the length < 4 check
